cover_caption: EgoScale: two-stage framework — 20,854 hours egocentric human video pretraining + lightweight aligned mid-training
source_title: EgoScale: Scaling Dexterous Manipulation with Diverse Egocentric Human Data

ai_summary.one_liner: 20,854 hours egocentric human video pretraining discovers scaling law (R²=0.9983) for robot dexterous manipulation
ai_summary.problem: Robot dexterity data is scarce; can massive egocentric human video scale robot manipulation performance?
ai_summary.solution: Two-stage framework: large-scale human pretraining (20K hours) + lightweight aligned human-robot mid-training with flow-based VLA
ai_summary.key_result: 54% avg success rate improvement; scaling law R²=0.9983; one-shot shirt folding 88%; cross-embodiment 30%p improvement
limitations: No saturation observed — more data needed

figures.caption: EgoScale's two-stage human-to-robot learning framework. VLA pretrained on 20,854 hours of egocentric human video, then lightweight aligned mid-training with human-robot play data.
figures.caption_ko: EgoScale의 2단계 인간-로봇 학습 프레임워크. 20,854시간 자기중심 인간 비디오로 VLA를 사전학습한 후, 정렬된 인간-로봇 play 데이터로 경량 중간학습을 수행한다.

figures.caption: Scaling behavior analysis. (Left) Training curves for 1K-20K hour datasets. (Center) Optimal convergence loss showing log-linear relationship (R²=0.9983). (Right) Downstream robot performance improving with data scale.
figures.caption_ko: 스케일링 행동 분석. (왼쪽) 1K~20K 시간 데이터셋의 학습 곡선. (중앙) 최적 수렴 loss의 로그-선형 관계 (R²=0.9983). (오른쪽) 데이터 규모 증가에 따른 로봇 성능 향상.
